architecture.md-182-| `skillSlugs` | Per-link skill list. `null` means "inherit the agent's `defaultSkillSlugs`"; an array replaces it entirely. The skill picker (`SkillPickerSection`) shows org skills and system skills side by side. |
architecture.md-183-| `customInstructions` | Appended to the agent's `additionalPrompt` at run time. Scoped per subaccount — lets an org agent speak the subaccount's language without org-wide edits. Max 10 000 chars. |
architecture.md:184:| `tokenBudgetPerRun` / `maxToolCallsPerRun` / `timeoutSeconds` / `maxCostPerRunCents` / `maxLlmCallsPerRun` | Hard ceilings enforced by `runCostBreaker` and the execution loop. `maxCostPerRunCents` plugs into the shared cost circuit breaker (`server/lib/runCostBreaker.ts`). Callers: Slack + Whisper via `assertWithinRunBudget` (cost_aggregates rollup); the LLM router via the direct-ledger sibling `assertWithinRunBudgetFromLedger` (reads `llm_requests` to avoid aggregation lag — Hermes Tier 1 Phase C, `tasks/hermes-audit-tier-1-spec.md` §7.4.1). |
architecture.md-185-| `heartbeatEnabled` / `heartbeatIntervalHours` / `heartbeatOffsetMinutes` | Per-subaccount schedule. Overrides the org agent's heartbeat so different clients can run at different cadences / offsets. |
architecture.md-186-| `scheduleCron` / `scheduleEnabled` / `scheduleTimezone` | Cron-based schedule (alternative to heartbeat interval). Schedule changes go through `agentScheduleService.updateSchedule` — **never mutate these columns directly**, or the pg-boss cron registration drifts from the DB. |
--
architecture.md-529-- `agent_execution_events` — durable typed event log, keyed `UNIQUE (run_id, sequence_number)`. Sequence allocation is atomic against `agent_runs.next_event_seq` via a single `UPDATE … RETURNING` — no MAX scan. Every event carries `source_service`, `duration_since_run_start_ms`, an event-typed `payload jsonb`, and optional `linked_entity_{type,id}` (null-together, enforced by both the service validator and a DB `CHECK` constraint as belt-and-braces). `permissionMask` is **never persisted** — it's computed at read time from the caller's current permissions, closing the privilege-drift hazard where a revoked grant would still read `canEdit: true` on historical rows.
architecture.md-530-- `agent_run_prompts` — fully-assembled `system_prompt` + `user_prompt` + `tool_definitions` + `layer_attributions` per run assembly. Closes the audit gap where only `systemPromptTokens` (count, not content) was persisted. Surrogate `id uuid PK` lets `agent_execution_events.linked_entity_id` point at prompts like any other entity; the `(run_id, assembly_number)` UNIQUE is still the drilldown key.
architecture.md:531:- `agent_run_llm_payloads` — full request + response per `llm_requests.id` (1:1). Keyed by `llm_request_id`; carries a nullable denormalised `run_id` FK to `agent_runs` (null for non-agent callers — skill-analyzer, config assistant) for cheap per-run scans. Written through the redaction → tool-policy → size-cap pipeline in `server/services/agentRunPayloadWriter.ts::buildPayloadRow`. Defence-in-depth: pattern-based redaction in `server/lib/redaction.ts` scrubs bearer tokens + common secret shapes; per-tool `payloadPersistencePolicy: 'full' | 'args-redacted' | 'args-never-persisted'` lets credential-handling skills opt into stricter persistence. `redacted_fields` records pattern hits; `modifications` records everything else (truncation, tool-policy substitution) with original field sizes. Hard per-row cap at `AGENT_EXECUTION_LOG_MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES` (1 MB default) with greatest-first truncation — TOAST compresses what's left transparently.
architecture.md-532-
architecture.md-533-Sequence-allocation semantics:
--
architecture.md-543-- Per-event edit links inherit from each linked entity's existing edit permission (WORKSPACE_MANAGE, SKILLS_MANAGE, AGENTS_EDIT, etc.). System-managed agents and immutable entities (`prompt`, `llm_request`, `action`) always return `canEdit: false`.
architecture.md-544-
architecture.md:545:Read path — `GET /api/agent-runs/:runId/events?fromSeq=&limit=` returns a 1000-row-capped page; `GET …/prompts/:assemblyNumber` returns one assembly; `GET …/llm-payloads/:llmRequestId` is stricter (AGENTS_EDIT) and double-gates via both the `llm_requests.run_id` upstream pre-check AND the denormalised `agent_run_llm_payloads.run_id` secondary check. Live stream via the existing `agent-run:${runId}` socket room and new `agent-run:execution-event` event kind; socket `join:agent-run` runs the full `resolveAgentRunVisibility` AGENTS_VIEW check (not just org-membership) so the push channel matches the pull channel's gate. Client dedup uses the existing 500-entry LRU on `${runId}:${sequenceNumber}:${eventType}` event IDs.
architecture.md-546-
architecture.md-547-Client timeline (`AgentRunLivePage`) — snapshot + socket merge keyed on event `id` + sorted by `sequenceNumber`. Monotonic guard drops socket events with `sequenceNumber <= lastSeenSeq`; sliding-window cap at `TIMELINE_WINDOW_SIZE = 2000` bounds UI memory while the server-side snapshot endpoint remains the authoritative history. A cap-reached banner surfaces on any timeline that contains a `run.event_limit_reached` event, with a "View run trace →" deep-link for the full LLM ledger. Process-local counters `sequenceGapsTotal` + `sequenceCollisionsTotal` (exported via `getAgentRunLiveClientMetrics()`) complement the per-incident `console.warn` lines for diagnosing upstream invariant breaks.

architecture.md-1336-### Layer 1 — Postgres RLS policies
architecture.md-1337-
architecture.md:1338:**Org-level (migrations 0079–0081):** 10 tables protected: `tasks`, `actions`, `agent_runs`, `agent_run_snapshots`, `review_items`, `review_audit_records`, `workspace_memories`, `llm_requests`, `audit_events`. Each has a `CREATE POLICY` keyed on `current_setting('app.organisation_id', true)`. Migration `0188` extends this to `llm_requests_archive` with the same org-scoped policy + `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`; the nightly retention job routes through `withAdminConnection` + `SET LOCAL ROLE admin_role` to perform the cross-org move (see LLM router contract → LLM ledger retention).
architecture.md-1339-
architecture.md-1340-**Principal-scoped (migrations 0167–0169):** P3B extends org-level RLS with visibility predicates on canonical data and integration tables. Tables: `integration_connections`, `integration_ingestion_stats`, `canonical_fields`, `canonical_row_versions`, `canonical_metric_history`, `canonical_row_subaccount_scopes`, `service_principals`, `teams`, `team_members`, `delegation_grants`, `agent_runs` (extended). Policies enforce:

architecture.md-1608-| `getRunCostCents(runId)` | `cost_aggregates` (async rollup) | `sendToSlackService`, `transcribeAudioService` |
architecture.md-1609-| `assertWithinRunBudget(ctx)` | `cost_aggregates` via `getRunCostCents` | `sendToSlackService`, `transcribeAudioService` |
architecture.md:1610:| `getRunCostCentsFromLedger(runId)` | `llm_requests` directly | `llmRouter.routeCall` |
architecture.md:1611:| `assertWithinRunBudgetFromLedger(ctx)` | `llm_requests` via `getRunCostCentsFromLedger` | `llmRouter.routeCall` |
architecture.md-1612-
architecture.md-1613-The direct-ledger pair exists because `cost_aggregates` is updated asynchronously by `routerJobService.enqueueAggregateUpdate`, so a rollup-based read lags by up to one aggregation interval. The LLM router is the dominant cost surface; it cannot tolerate that lag. Slack and Whisper stay on the rollup path because their per-call magnitudes dwarf the lag and their concurrency profiles are low. The ledger helper takes `insertedLedgerRowId` as a REQUIRED parameter and fails closed on null or row-not-visible — a structural guarantee that a future refactor cannot re-order the call above the ledger insert. See `tasks/hermes-audit-tier-1-spec.md` §7.3.1 / §7.4.1.
--
architecture.md-1624-
architecture.md-1625-- `totalCostCents` — from `cost_aggregates` (includes failed-call cost for accounting completeness).
architecture.md:1626:- `llmCallCount`, `totalTokensIn`, `totalTokensOut`, `callSiteBreakdown: { app, worker }` — from the archive-safe `llm_requests_all` view under a success/partial filter.
architecture.md-1627-
architecture.md-1628-The asymmetry between `totalCostCents` (rollup, includes failures) and the new fields (ledger, success/partial only) is intentional; the H1 deferred follow-up in `tasks/todo.md` proposes adding an explicit `successfulCostCents` field to remove the UI-interpretation trap.

Read-only file preview is rendered server-side via `/render` — the client never constructs the file body.

It is the only caller of the Studio tools; org agents do not get access to Studio endpoints (blocked by `requireSystemAdmin`).

architecture.md-2539-   - Acquires a `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` lock on the parent `agent_run` row.

architecture.md-2669-

--
architecture.md-2739-The gap the tracker couldn't close on its own: `providerAdapter.call()` succeeds (provider has billed and generated tokens) → `db.insert(llmRequests)` fails for any reason (DB blip, constraint violation, crash) → caller retries under the same `idempotencyKey` → the pre-dispatch idempotency check finds no row → router dispatches a second concurrent call → **double-bill at the provider with no ledger trace of the first success**. No LLM provider currently ships a request-level dedup header.
architecture.md-2740-
architecture.md:2741:The `llm_requests.status` enum has a provisional value `'started'` (migration `0190_llm_requests_started_status.sql` — partial index on `created_at WHERE status = 'started'`). Flow:
architecture.md-2742-
architecture.md-2743-1. **Atomic idempotency check + provisional INSERT.** `llmRouter.routeCall` step 4+7 runs a single `db.transaction`. It does `SELECT … FOR UPDATE` on `idempotencyKey`; if a `'success'` row exists it returns the cached response; if a `'started'` row exists it returns an `inflight` marker; otherwise it INSERTs a fresh `'started'` row inside the same transaction (with `onConflictDoUpdate` on any non-success state so a retry after terminal-error resets `createdAt` to `now()` — preventing the revived row from being immediately sweep-eligible). A concurrent second caller blocks on the unique-constraint conflict; when the first tx commits, the second's own `FOR UPDATE` returns the `'started'` row and correctly takes the reconciliation branch.
--
architecture.md-2810-`'organisation' \| 'subaccount' \| 'run' \| 'agent' \| 'task_type' \| 'provider' \| 'platform' \| 'execution_phase' \| 'source_type' \| 'feature_tag'`
architecture.md-2811-
architecture.md:2812:The last two — `source_type` and `feature_tag` — were added by spec §6.2 so platform-overhead, per-feature, and per-source-type rollups don't require live scans of `llm_requests`. `cost_aggregates` is NOT RLS-protected (it carries aggregated totals, not PII), which keeps the existing admin usage routes working without bypass wiring.
architecture.md-2813-
architecture.md-2814-### LLM ledger retention (spec §12.4 / §15.5)
architecture.md-2815-
architecture.md:2816:`llm_requests` rows older than `env.LLM_LEDGER_RETENTION_MONTHS` (default `12`) are moved to `llm_requests_archive` by the nightly `maintenance:llm-ledger-archive` pg-boss job (`server/jobs/llmLedgerArchiveJob.ts`, 03:45 UTC):
architecture.md-2817-
architecture.md-2818-- **10k-row chunks** — bounded transaction size keeps lock footprint small.
architecture.md-2819-- **Atomic move** — one CTE chain `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED → INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING → DELETE RETURNING`. A row is either in the live table OR the archive, never both and never neither.
architecture.md:2820:- **Admin-bypass RLS** — both `llm_requests` and `llm_requests_archive` have `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`. The job runs under `withAdminConnection({ source: 'llmLedgerArchiveJob' }, …)` + `SET LOCAL ROLE admin_role` (BYPASSRLS). Direct `db.transaction` would fail closed.
architecture.md-2821-- **Cutoff math** is pure in `llmLedgerArchiveJobPure.ts::computeArchiveCutoff` so retention behaviour is test-pinned.
architecture.md-2822-
--
architecture.md-2825-### System P&L page (spec §11)
architecture.md-2826-
architecture.md:2827:`/system/llm-pnl` is the one UI that is intentionally cross-tenant. Routes (`server/routes/systemPnl.ts`) enforce `requireSystemAdmin`; the service (`server/services/systemPnlService.ts`) runs every read inside `adminRead(reason, fn)` — a thin wrapper over `withAdminConnection({source:'systemPnlService', reason}, tx => { await tx.execute(sql\`SET LOCAL ROLE admin_role\`); return fn(tx); })`. Cross-org reads without the role switch fail closed against the FORCE RLS policy on `llm_requests` + archive.
architecture.md-2828-
architecture.md-2829-Data split:
architecture.md-2830-
architecture.md-2831-- **Scalar KPIs + per-org / per-subaccount rollups** read `cost_aggregates` (sub-100 ms, no live scan).
architecture.md:2832:- **Source-type / provider+model rollups, top calls, call detail** read `llm_requests` live — bounded by the indexed `billing_month` scan.
architecture.md-2833-- **Daily trend** reads `cost_aggregates` (`entity_type='platform'`, `period_type='daily'`).
architecture.md-2834-

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md-325-3. **Per-job double-invocation regression test.** Each job gets `server/jobs/__tests__/<job>.idempotency.test.ts` exercising:
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md-326-   - **Sequential double-invocation:** call the job twice in a row; assert state matches single-invocation, no duplicate side effects.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md:327:   - **Parallel double-invocation:** start two invocations concurrently via `Promise.all([job(), job()])`; assert exactly one performs work (the other returns no-op or yields the same final state). Use `pg_sleep(0.1)` or test-controlled latency to widen the race window deterministically.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md-328-   - **Mid-execution failure:** simulate a transient throw inside the work block; on retry, assert no partial state is left behind.

tasks/todo.md:507:- [ ] **F21 / §7.4 / §12.16 — Rule 3 "Check now" trigger mechanism OR Rule 3 removal.** Rule 3 in the heartbeat gate depends on a "Check now" button/API that does NOT exist in the current codebase. Two options: (a) add a new `subaccount_agents.check_now_requested_at timestamptz NULL` column + `POST /api/subaccount-agents/:id/check-now` route + admin UI button (extra scope for a "cheap observation fix"), OR (b) drop Rule 3 from v1 and ship the gate with 3 rules. Agent recommendation: (b). Human to confirm.
tasks/todo.md:821:- [ ] **#9 — Incident correlation clusters.** Group related incidents via `correlation_id` + `affected_resource_*` into logical clusters so operators see "one underlying cause" instead of N fingerprints. Requires a correlation-computation pass (batch job or on-write) and a cluster-summary surface in the admin UI. Phase 1 scope — out of scope for Phase 0/0.5.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md:103:**`prefer_existing_primitives_over_new_ones: yes`** — every Phase 1B refactor reuses `withOrgTx` / `getOrgScopedDb` from `server/instrumentation.ts` and `server/lib/orgScopedDb.ts` (org-scoped helpers), plus `withAdminConnection` from `server/lib/adminDbConnection.ts` (admin / system-scoped helper). The corrective migration in §4.1 mirrors the pattern of migration `0213_fix_cached_context_rls.sql` (the canonical reference for repairing already-broken RLS). The principal-context propagation work in §5.4 reuses `withPrincipalContext` and the `fromOrgId()` shim that already exists. **The new files this spec introduces are mechanical service-tier homes for code being relocated out of routes/lib (§4.2) plus one narrow new shared primitive in Phase 5 §8.1 (`server/services/rateLimitStoreService.ts`) — no new architectural primitives or service layers.** The Phase 5 §8.1 primitive has an explicit "why not reuse" paragraph in §8.1; the §4.2 service files are pure relocations (no new abstractions, no new public API surface). Where a fix appears to need a primitive that does not yet exist, the spec must document why reuse and extension were both insufficient — and the reviewer treats absence of that justification as directional.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md:315:2. Every Phase 1B service-layer change (§4.2) and Phase 1C cross-org-write guard (§4.3) is staged in the same PR as the migration. The migration's `WITH CHECK` enforcement will reject writes whose session var is unset; staging the service-tier work in the same PR bounds the in-PR fail-closed window to the brief moment between migration-apply and route-refactor-merge inside that PR.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md:1954:- **Prefer existing primitives over new abstractions.** Every Phase 1–4 fix invokes existing primitives — `withOrgTx`, `withAdminConnection`, `withPrincipalContext`, `fromOrgId`, `llmRouter`, `canonicalDataService`, `resolveSubaccount`. The §4.2 new service files are pure relocations (DB access moves from routes/lib into the service tier; no new public API). The single new architectural primitive this spec introduces is Phase 5 §8.1's `server/services/rateLimitStoreService.ts` — justified inline in §8.1 against the alternatives (Redis would be a new infrastructure dependency; the existing in-memory `Map<>`-based limiters do not meet the multi-process correctness requirement). New primitives have a high evidentiary bar (per `prefer_existing_primitives_over_new_ones: yes`); every other section of this spec satisfies that bar by extending or reusing the listed primitives.
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md:94:2. **Service-side migration.** For each `canonicalDataService` method, change the signature to accept `PrincipalContext` as the first parameter; derive `organisationId` (and `subaccountId` where applicable) from it internally. Read methods that accept `accountId` keep `accountId` as a separate parameter — `PrincipalContext` describes the requesting principal, not the target row. **No deprecation shims** (project is pre-production per `docs/spec-context.md` framing).

codex
## Cross-cutting

1. The document metadata is self-contradictory: it says `Created: 2026-04-26` and `Last revised: 2026-04-25` at [spec lines 3-4](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md). Fix by setting `Last revised` to the actual latest edit date or changing `Created` if this was backfilled from an earlier draft. Severity: `minor`.

2. Several items violate the stated testing posture of `runtime_tests: pure_function_only` and “static gates primary,” which the source spec restates explicitly at [source spec line 101](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md) and architecture repeats at [architecture.md lines 1468-1499](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/architecture.md). The drift shows up in A1 DB/session-var tests, A2 write-path tests, B2 parallel job regression tests, F2 integration/multi-process tests, and H1 service tests; replace those with pure-helper extraction + pure tests, static gates, or manual smoke steps where live DB behavior is the thing being checked. Severity: `critical`.

## A1

1. The proposed hardened gate still only proves “this file has at least one `fromOrgId(` call,” not “every `canonicalDataService` call site was migrated,” so a file can keep legacy calls and still pass. Ref: A1 step 4 / acceptance at spec lines 96-104, plus the current gate’s file-level strategy in [scripts/verify-principal-context-propagation.sh lines 7-9, 24-45](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/scripts/verify-principal-context-propagation.sh). Fix by keying the gate to actual call expressions or banning legacy signatures directly, not by checking for a single helper invocation somewhere in the file. Severity: `important`.

2. A1’s annotation contract conflicts with C4’s proposed end state for `actionRegistry.ts`: after A1 changes the gate to target files importing `canonicalDataService`, `actionRegistry.ts` should drop the dead `fromOrgId` import, not keep an `@principal-context-import-only` exemption. Ref: A1 lines 96-103, C4 lines 499-506, and [server/config/actionRegistry.ts lines 2-4](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/server/config/actionRegistry.ts). Fix by making C4 say “remove the dead import/comment after A1” unless the file truly imports `canonicalDataService`. Severity: `important`.

## A2

1. The migration-hook path is wrong: A2 says the hook watches `server/db/migrations/*.sql`, but this repo’s migrations live under top-level `migrations/*.sql`. Ref: A2 line 155 and the actual tree under `migrations/`. Fix by changing the watched path to `migrations/*.sql` everywhere in A2. Severity: `critical`.

2. The runtime-guard mechanism is load-bearing but unnamed in implementable terms: “Hook into Drizzle’s executor middleware” is specified, but the repo has no existing Drizzle query-middleware seam to attach to, and the proposed file path `server/lib/db/rlsBoundaryGuard.ts` does not match current DB-helper placement (`server/lib/*` and `server/db/*`). Ref: A2 lines 136-155 plus [server/lib/orgScopedDb.ts](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/server/lib/orgScopedDb.ts) / [server/db](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/server/db/index.ts). Fix by naming the exact interception point already available in this codebase, or by scoping A2 to the schema-diff gate and migration hook until a real write-hook seam exists. Severity: `important`.

3. The exemption mechanism is internally inconsistent: step 1 says the gate may inspect “live schema (or parse migrations),” but the exemption lives in a Drizzle schema-file comment `@rls-not-applicable`, which neither live schema nor SQL migrations can see. Ref: A2 lines 145-158. Fix by choosing one source of truth for exemptions, e.g. an explicit allowlist/manifest entry consumed by both the gate and runtime guard. Severity: `important`.

## A3

1. A3 tells implementers they can create `const db = getOrgScopedDb(...)` “at the top of the module” and can call `withOrgTx(organisationId, ...)`, but both are invalid against the current primitives: `getOrgScopedDb()` throws outside an active org-scoped context and `withOrgTx` takes an `OrgTxContext` object, not a bare string. Ref: A3 lines 201-202, [server/lib/orgScopedDb.ts lines 30-42](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/server/lib/orgScopedDb.ts), and [server/instrumentation.ts lines 152-173](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/server/instrumentation.ts). Fix by requiring function-local `getOrgScopedDb()` calls inside already-scoped executions, and if `withOrgTx` is needed, showing the real `OrgTxContext` shape. Severity: `critical`.

2. A3 says “No new gate needed” because an existing gate already discourages raw `db` imports outside the data layer, but the actual gate explicitly allowlists `server/services/**`, so it will not catch these service-level regressions. Ref: A3 line 204 and [scripts/verify-rls-contract-compliance.sh lines 9-13, 40-48](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/scripts/verify-rls-contract-compliance.sh). Fix by either naming the real existing enforcement gap or adding a narrow service-layer rule instead of claiming it is already covered. Severity: `important`.

## B2

1. The mitigation says to run flaky tests with `Jest --repeats 10`, but this repo is not a Jest runner; `package.json` routes tests through shell scripts and `tsx`. Ref: B2 risk lines 341-343 and [package.json lines 23-28](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/package.json). Fix by replacing the Jest instruction with the actual repo harness, e.g. a small repeat loop in `scripts/run-all-unit-tests.sh` or repeated `tsx` invocation for the relevant file. Severity: `important`.

## D3

1. D3’s proposed `@no-read-path` annotation does not match how the current gate works, because `verify-skill-read-paths.sh` is only doing raw `readPath:` line counts and has no per-entry parser. Ref: D3 lines 636-646 and [scripts/verify-skill-read-paths.sh lines 14-25](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/scripts/verify-skill-read-paths.sh). Fix by either committing to rewrite the gate to parse action entries plus exemptions, or by limiting D3 to real `readPath` additions/removals only. Severity: `important`.

## E2

1. E2 invents `scripts/baselines/integration-reference.txt`, but the repo’s guard baseline system is centralized in `scripts/guard-baselines.json` via `check_baseline()`. Ref: E2 line 743 and [scripts/lib/guard-utils.sh lines 134-161](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/scripts/lib/guard-utils.sh). Fix by using the existing baseline store and naming the exact guard id to update, not introducing a parallel baseline format. Severity: `important`.

## F2

1. F2 contradicts the source spec’s existing primitive decision: the source spec already names `server/services/rateLimitStoreService.ts` as the shared TTL/key-value primitive for this defect class, but the follow-up spec invents a new `kvStoreWithTtlService.ts`. Ref: F2 lines 837-868, [tasks/todo.md line 953](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/tasks/todo.md), and [source spec §8.1 references around lines 1300-1346 and 1719](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md). Fix by making F2 explicitly extend/consume `rateLimitStoreService` if Phase 5A lands first, or by adding a “why reuse/extension is insufficient” paragraph if a second primitive is truly intended. Severity: `critical`.

2. F2 pre-allocates migration `0228_*`, which conflicts with the source spec’s explicit merge-time numbering rule for new migrations. Ref: F2 line 838 versus [source spec lines 146-148 and 1313](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md). Fix by changing `0228_*` to `migrations/<NNNN>_...sql` and pointing at the existing numbering rule. Severity: `important`.

## G1

1. G1 is directionally inconsistent and mechanically contradictory: it requires staging-only steps (“Take a snapshot of the staging DB”, “First run passes against current staging”) in a codebase whose context says no staging posture, and it labels the work “read-only verification against staging” even though the script explicitly performs writes. Ref: G1 lines 898, 906, 913 and [docs/spec-context.md line 38](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/spec-context.md). Fix by replacing “staging” with “local dev or any disposable database,” and by correcting the risk language to acknowledge fresh-DB writes plus explicit test writes under RLS. Severity: `critical`.
tokens used
198,627
## Cross-cutting

1. The document metadata is self-contradictory: it says `Created: 2026-04-26` and `Last revised: 2026-04-25` at [spec lines 3-4](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-26-audit-remediation-followups-spec.md). Fix by setting `Last revised` to the actual latest edit date or changing `Created` if this was backfilled from an earlier draft. Severity: `minor`.

2. Several items violate the stated testing posture of `runtime_tests: pure_function_only` and “static gates primary,” which the source spec restates explicitly at [source spec line 101](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-codebase-audit-remediation-spec.md) and architecture repeats at [architecture.md lines 1468-1499](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/architecture.md). The drift shows up in A1 DB/session-var tests, A2 write-path tests, B2 parallel job regression tests, F2 integration/multi-process tests, and H1 service tests; replace those with pure-helper extraction + pure tests, static gates, or manual smoke steps where live DB behavior is the thing being checked. Severity: `critical`.

## A1

1. The proposed hardened gate still only proves “this file has at least one `fromOrgId(` call,” not “every `canonicalDataService` call site was migrated,” so a file can keep legacy calls and still pass. Ref: A1 step 4 / acceptance at spec lines 96-104, plus the current gate’s file-level strategy in [scripts/verify-principal-context-propagation.sh lines 7-9, 24-45](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/scripts/verify-principal-context-propagation.sh). Fix by keying the gate to actual call expressions or banning legacy signatures directly, not by checking for a single helper invocation somewhere in the file. Severity: `important`.

2. A1’s annotation contract conflicts with C4’s proposed end state for `actionRegistry.ts`: after A1 changes the gate to target files importing `canonicalDataService`, `actionRegistry.ts` should drop the dead `fromOrgId` import, not keep an `@principal-context-import-only` exemption. Ref: A1 lines 96-103, C4 lines 499-506, and [server/config/actionRegistry.ts lines 2-4](C:/Files/Projects/automation-v1/server/config/actionRegistry.ts). Fix by making C4 say “remove the dead import/comment after A1” unless the file truly imports `canonicalDataService`. Severity: `important`.
